title: Boston broke the heat record at 96 degrees. Here’s how long the steamy weather will last.
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Boston reached a record high of 96 degrees, marking the hottest May 19 on record since 1944.
Real relief from the heat is expected by Thursday, with temperatures only slightly cooler on Wednesday.
Boston tied for the second hottest spring day on record, with storms expected during the evening.

title: 3 stabbed, 2 arrested in chaotic scene at R.I. town beach, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Three people suffered minor stab wounds at Narragansett Town Beach, Rhode Island; police received calls about the stabbings at 3 p.m.
Two people were arrested for unrelated disturbances involving simple assault and disorderly conduct at the same location.
Authorities from multiple local departments responded, and the stabbing incidents remain under active investigation with no suspects identified.

title: After auction fizzles out, Karen Read’s SUV listed for nearly $100K at Dedham dealership
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Karen Read's 2021 Lexus LX 570 is listed for $100,000 at Autobahn USA Dedham after a failed auction.
The SUV, involved in Read's murder trials, was initially auctioned in Woburn but sold privately due to low attendance.
The vehicle requires repairs, including a dead battery and shattered dashboard, and has a missing taillight.

title: Racist and antisemitic graffiti investigated at Natick elementary school
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Racist and antisemitic graffiti was found on exterior walls of Lilja Elementary School in Natick.
Officials stated the graffiti included a swastika and an anti-Black slur, which were removed immediately.
Natick Police and Public Schools pledged to investigate and prevent future hate incidents.

title: Belichick: Tom Brady leaving Patriots in 2020 was ‘absolutely the right thing for him to do’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bill Belichick stated on Fox News that Tom Brady's departure from the Patriots in 2020 was beneficial for his career.
Brady led the Tampa Bay Buccaneers to a Super Bowl win and performed at a high level until his 2022 retirement.
Belichick attributed Brady's departure to the Patriots' deteriorating roster and acknowledged their relationship continued positively post-split.

title: Hot dogs in historic paintings? Kayem and the MFA imagine exactly that.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Kayem Hot Dogs partnered with the Museum of Fine Arts to incorporate hot dogs into iconic American paintings.
The art series, debuting June 4, will travel across New England with stops including Fenway Park and Faneuil Hall.
Kayem launches a Cannon Fire Hot Dog for the campaign, sold as a $2.50 four-pack in New England.

title: Patriots’ Brenden Schooler shares his opinion on grass vs. turf as Gillette Stadium undergoes changes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Gillette Stadium replaced its artificial turf with natural grass for the FIFA World Cup compliance.
Patriots' Brenden Schooler prefers grass, citing reduced injury risk and less physical strain compared to turf.
NFLPA data shows 92% of players favor grass over turf due to lower injury rates on natural surfaces.

title: A man died after getting stuck at the bottom of an MBTA escalator. Authorities are still investigating.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Steven McCluskey died after becoming trapped in an escalator at MBTA's Davis Station on February 27.
The escalator was stopped over 20 minutes after McCluskey fell, with several bystanders passing by without helping.
The incident is under investigation by the Middlesex County District Attorney’s Office to determine cause and manner of death.

title: ‘Immature’ and ‘out of control’: How an inside baseball feud is roiling the Boston City Council
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Councilors Erin Murphy and Sharon Durkan clash over procedural blocks on late-file measures in Boston City Council meetings.
Murphy accuses Durkan of selectively obstructing measures, impacting the council's work and public perception.
Durkan argues blocking late-file matters ensures compliance with open meeting laws, reserved for emergency use only.

title: Belmont hair salon gutted by fire that also destroyed ice cream shop
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A fire in Belmont destroyed Lucy Danger Hair salon and Moozy's Ice Cream Emporium last Thursday.
Stylist Lucy Ankner plans to leave the salon industry, viewing the fire as a sign for a career change.
A GoFundMe was launched to support displaced Lucy Danger Hair stylists, with clients and supporters responding generously.
